title: Nvidia and Microsoft tease "a new era of PC" ahead of Computex 2026 coordinated social media posts could indicate that rumored N1X laptops will be Windows on Arm systems
summary: Nvidia and Microsoft are teasing a new era of PC, possibly featuring Windows on Arm systems in rumored N1X laptops, which could enable new local AI experiences, such as beyond Copilot+.
insight: Nvidia and Microsoft's collaboration could revolutionize AI capabilities on PCs.

title: TikTok owner ByteDance is reportedly developing its own custom AI CPUs company looks to ease China's dependence on US chipmakers
summary: TikTok owner Bytedance is reportedly developing its own custom CPUs to reduce costs and dependence on US chipmakers.
insight: Bytedance's custom CPUs aim to mitigate China's reliance on foreign technology.

title: Cooler Master is bringing active cooling to DDR5 RAM, promising up to 15-degree temperature drops 'MasterDIMM' combines G.SKILL memory with a built-in fan, kits run up to 128GB
summary: Cooler Master and G.Skill launch 'MasterDIMM' DDR5 memory sticks with built-in fans, reducing temperatures by up to 15 degrees. The MasterDIMM features a stylish black and gold heatsink with RGB stripes. Kits can hold up to 128GB of memory.
insight: Integrated cooling can improve system performance and reduce overheating risks

title: Hands-on with Corsair's 3200D RS ARGB Mid-tower PC Case: Budget chassis includes three fans and doesn t empty your wallet
summary: The Corsair 3200D RS ARGB mid-tower PC case offers strong airflow, supports large GPUs and radiators, and comes with three built-in fans at an affordable price under $80.
insight: Affordable PC case with robust cooling and large GPU support.

title: Epic Games Tim Sweeney slams Valve over Steam Deck price hikes mocks founder Gabe Newell over rising costs of megayachts
summary: Epic Games' Tim Sweeney criticized Valve CEO Gabe Newell over Steam Deck price hikes and his expensive megayacht collection on social media. The comparison was made to highlight the Steam Deck's significant price increases compared to other gaming consoles. The post sparked a public debate about the cost of gaming hardware.
insight: Valve's Steam Deck price hikes raise competition and consumer concern

title: Amazon unveils 'Resilient Network Graphs' data center network that cuts hardware by 69% and boosts throughput by 33% now the default for most AWS workloads
summary: Amazon Web Services (AWS) has unveiled 'Resilient Network Graphs' (RNG), a new data center network architecture that significantly reduces hardware usage and boosts throughput. RNG delivers 33% higher throughput, cuts network power consumption by 40%, and uses 69% fewer devices. It is now the default for most AWS workloads.
insight: Significant cost savings and efficiency gains in cloud infrastructure management.

title: Bambu Lab teases new A2L 3D printer June 1 launch hints at a massive, budget-friendly bed slinger
summary: Bambu Lab is teasing a new A2L 3D printer with a June 1 launch, sparking speculation about its features.
insight: Potential massive, budget-friendly 3D printer could disrupt the market.

title: Acer unveils its first Ryzen 9 9955X3D gaming laptop refreshed Nitro 16 joins new Predator Helios 18 AI and streaming-only Nitro Blaze Link handheld
summary: Acer unveils new gaming laptops, including its first Ryzen 9 9955HX3D model, and a handheld device. The Nitro 16 features triple PCIe 5.0 storage, while the Predator Helios 18 boasts AI capabilities. These devices are showcased at Computex.
insight: Acer expands gaming laptop lineup with high-performance Ryzen and AI features.

title: Acer cranks its latest Predator gaming monitor up to 1,000 Hz Model joins lineup with QD-OLED panels and an immersive 3D display
summary: Acer introduces a high-end gaming monitor with a 1,000 Hz refresh rate, QD-OLED panels, and immersive 3D capabilities. The monitor joins Acer's lineup with its advanced features. The new monitor is set to be showcased at Computex.
insight: Unmatched gaming performance and immersion with cutting-edge display technology.

title: PCMFlow722 library enables two-way real-time HD voice over ESP-NOW with G.722 audio codec
summary: The PCMFlow722 library enables two-way real-time HD voice over ESP-NOW on ESP32 boards, utilizing the G.722 codec for 7 kHz audio at 16 kHz sampling.
insight: HD voice over ESP-NOW achieves 7 kHz at 64 kbps with G.722 codec.

title: Microchip dsPIC33CK low-cost motor control MCU sells for $0.51 and up
summary: Microchip has released the dsPIC33CK Value Line, a low-cost 16-bit DSC series for motor control applications, offering up to 100 MIPS and high-resolution PWM.
insight: Cost-effective motor control MCU for industrial and automotive applications.

title: Here s more Sensors Converge 2026 scoop from ST and Microchip
summary: ST and Microchip will showcase robotic hand and hand tracking tech, along with an ST-Nvidia humanoid proof of concept at Sensors Converge 2026.
insight: Advancements in robotics and AI enable more precise human-machine interactions.

title: Startup Zone at Sensors Converge features Upbeat and others
summary: Upbeat Technologies will showcase its AI-powered MEMS vibration sensors at Sensors Converge. These sensors are claimed to be the world's smallest. This innovation is expected to revolutionize various industries.
insight: Advancements in AI-powered sensing technology enhance industrial precision and efficiency

title: Are you actually using AI on your PC?
summary: AI is ubiquitous, but its integration into everyday tools is questionable.
insight: Availability doesn't equal actual AI usage in daily applications.

title: Europe fines Temu $232 million over illegal and unsafe product listings
summary: The European Commission fined Temu $232 million for selling illegal goods that posed a risk to EU consumers. This marks the second fine ever against an international company under the Digital Services Act. Temu is a Chinese online marketplace for low-cost products.
insight: European consumer safety is compromised by unchecked cross-border online trade.

title: DRAM coolers make a comeback: G.Skill and Cooler Master unveil DDR5 kits with built-in fans
summary: G.Skill and Cooler Master release DDR5 kits with built-in fans, reducing memory temperatures by up to 15 C, ensuring sustained performance and improved signal integrity.
insight: Improved memory cooling for enhanced system reliability and performance stability.

title: Creative launches Sound Blaster AE-X for PC audio enthusiasts
summary: The Sound Blaster AE-X features an ESS Sabre DAC for improved sound quality.
insight: Enhanced audio clarity for PC gaming and music enthusiasts.

title: The ad-tracking industry is exposing US soldiers on the battlefield
summary: Centcom has acknowledged receiving reports of US personnel being targeted by adversaries using commercial location data.
insight: Adversary access to commercial data threatens US soldier safety and security.

title: New 7-Zip security flaw could put hundreds of millions of systems at risk
summary: A security flaw in 7-Zip could be exploited by cybercriminals to spread malware and compromise PCs. The bug affects how 7-Zip handles NTFS-based volume image files. This vulnerability puts hundreds of millions of systems at risk.
insight: Critical flaw in 7-Zip poses massive malware and data compromise risk.

title: Windows BitLocker exploit sparks messy feud between Microsoft and the researcher who exposed it
summary: A security researcher exposed a zero-day exploit in Windows BitLocker, allowing access to encrypted drives.
insight: Windows BitLocker encryption compromised by a publicly disclosed zero-day exploit.

title: Call of Duty: Modern Warfare 4 confirmed for October 23, brings the series to Korea
summary: Pre-orders open for Call of Duty: Modern Warfare 4, releasing on October 23.
insight: Call of Duty series expands to the Korean setting for the first time.

title: Google engineer arrested for using internal search data to win $1.2 million on Polymarket
summary: Google engineer Spagnuolo was arrested for using internal data to win $1.2 million on Polymarket, allegedly exploiting his inside knowledge to make bets on outcomes he already knew.
insight: Inside information can be misused for personal financial gain in the digital age.

title: TP-Link's first Wi-Fi 8 router is coming, if the FCC allows it
summary: TP-Link is preparing to release its first Wi-Fi 8 router, pending FCC approval. The company completed successful Wi-Fi 8 trials last year, validating data throughput and the Wi-Fi 8 beacon. This marks a significant step towards future wireless networking.
insight: Enhanced wireless networking for faster, more reliable internet connectivity.

title: Steam Deck sells out again despite $300 price hike, meanwhile Intel unveils new handheld chip to counter AMD's dominance
summary: The Steam Deck sells out after a $300 price hike, despite being less appealing. Intel responds by unveiling its Arc G3 series APUs, bringing its Panther Lake architecture to portable gaming. This move counters AMD's dominance in the market.
insight: Intel challenges AMD's handheld gaming PC market lead strongly

title: Wix lays off 1,000 workers as the AI jobs apocalypse keeps looking very real
summary: Wix lays off 1,000 workers in a 20% team reduction. CEO Avishai Abrahami described the decision as 'hard'. This move is part of a broader AI-driven job market shift.
insight: AI adoption accelerates job cuts in the tech industry
